working_on: Land routine-mode session pattern foundation per ADR 0051: third session mode for unattended cadence-driven Claude Code Routines, with two-layer architecture (master plan session authors target contract; engine loop session executes it), six-layer defense in depth, and Paperclip rejected in favor of subscription-funded approach.
declared_scope: Routine-mode session pattern foundation per approved plan at /Users/shanekidd/.claude/plans/1-prior-session-token-joyful-dijkstra.md. Land: (1) ADR 0051 plus matching MemPalace decision drawer; (2) routine-mode-operations.md Layer 1 ops doc; (3) auto_target.schema.md; (4) check_target.py predicate runner with 5 criterion types plus tests; (5) check_routine_scope.py for plan-vs-scope and staged-vs-scope checks plus tests; (6) session-build-lifecycle.md routine-mode branch; (7) validate.py routine-mode soft-warns; (8) pre-commit hook scope-check + master-plan-integrity; (9) CLAUDE.md third session mode; (10) STATE.md and operations/README.md updates; (11) adoption check end-to-end. Out of scope: Phase 5 master plan itself (S-0045), flipping routine from Manual to Hourly (user action post-S-0045), Anthropic count_tokens API integration (rejected by user). phase: NA-engine-apparatus.
outcome_summary: Routine-mode foundation landed end-to-end per ADR 0051. Six-layer anti-rogue defense operational. Mid-session user-directed expansion: separated /start-routine + routine-mode-lifecycle Skill from /start-engine + session-build-lifecycle (architecturally distinct lifecycles per five-difference test: boot, scope, commit, permission, shutdown; share only eager-claim ritual). Paperclip rejected (Anthropic April-2026 policy excludes third-party orchestration from subscription coverage; Paperclip's claude_local OAuth path incompatible with unattended dispatch). Phase 5 epistemology build-readiness gate (twice-displaced) reframed: gate report at engine/build_readiness/phase_5.md is now authored by S-0045's master plan session as part of producing the auto_target.json contract. Issue #3 filed (per ADR 0048): /start-engine boot should detect mid-routine in_progress and refuse to claim; surfaced when user asked about interactive-mid-routine concurrency; out of S-0044 scope. New tools: check_target.py (5 criterion types + predicate registry; 30 tests), check_routine_scope.py (plan/staged scope-check + master-plan integrity; segment-aware glob matching; 40 tests). New validate.py soft-warns: routine_no_target_reference, routine_issue_spam (12 tests). Pre-commit hook gains routine-mode scope-check section. Default-mode validator check count rises 21 → 23. Decision drawer captured for ADR 0051. Pre-existing tech debt fix-in-context: paired # type: ignore[import-not-found,unused-ignore] for psycopg in validate.py:1905-1906 (was masked by mypy cache at S-0043 close). Validator clean (23 checks, 0 hard-fails, 3 expected issue_collision soft-warns from Issues #1/#2/#3 keyword-matching the session's broad scope; persistent-warn threshold tripped at 3-of-5 for issue_collision — annotated as expected per scope breadth). pytest 516/516 passing (was 504; +82 new tests).

scope_delivery.explanation: All declared deliverables landed. One user-directed mid-session expansion: separating /start-routine + routine-mode-lifecycle Skill from /start-engine + session-build-lifecycle. Initial implementation bolted routine-mode into /start-engine via a step-0 detection branch in session-build-lifecycle.md; user pushback (verbatim: 'Why not separate start engine and start routine into different functions? Give the routine its function and don't have it overlap with start engine.') led to refactoring mid-session into two distinct entry points. The five-difference test (boot procedure, scope rules, commit posture, permission model, shutdown logic) confirmed they're architecturally separate, not branches. Paperclip trial-install was REJECTED (per the approved plan's stated supersession): API billing forced by Anthropic April-2026 policy; subscription doesn't cover unattended third-party orchestration; reasoning preserved in ADR 0051 Alternatives Considered. The Phase 5 epistemology build-readiness gate (twice-displaced before, third displacement here) is REFRAMED rather than deferred: its report at engine/build_readiness/phase_5.md is now authored by S-0045's master plan session as part of producing the auto_target.json contract — gate work materially preserved, becomes part of a richer master-plan deliverable. Adoption check via the existing pytest suite (516/516 passing including 82 new tests covering check_target, check_routine_scope, validate_routine_mode end-to-end with synthetic fixtures and real git repos) plus standalone CLI smoke tests of check_routine_scope --staged (no target → exit 0 silently) and check_target (no target → exit 1). Live routine fire deferred to user action after S-0045 lands a real target file. No descope or reorder.

user_directed_expansions.user_quote: Why not separate start engine and start routine into different functions? Give the routine its function and don't have it overlap with start engine.
user_directed_expansions.rationale: Routine-mode and interactive build differ in five ways (boot procedure, scope rules, commit posture, permission model, shutdown logic). Bolting them under one entry point conflated concerns. Two clean entry points share only the eager-claim ritual; everything else is mode-specific.
